title: AddNoteSheet — Inline Note Capture During Recording
problem: During recording, a band member sometimes wants to capture a longer thought than a stamp allows — 're-record the bridge', 'drummer was off-tempo here', 'strong verse'. Stamps are single-emoji; notes allow free text. But the note input must not disrupt the recording or require navigating away.
analysis:
AddNoteSheet is a lightweight bottom sheet that appears in response to the 'Add note' tap on the Recording screen. It slides up with the keyboard using `env(keyboard-inset-height)` for positioning — the sheet sits directly above the keyboard, not behind it.

Content:
- Single-line text input, placeholder 'What just happened?'
- Character counter appears at 100+ chars (text-xs text-muted, right-aligned). Max: 120 chars
- 'Save' and 'Cancel' buttons above the keyboard (within the sheet, ~44px tall)

On Save: annotation object `{ text, timestampMs }` appended to local state (flushed to Postgres on recording stop). Sheet dismisses. Recording continues uninterrupted. No haptic on save (avoid disrupting the take).

On Cancel or swipe-down: discards, recording continues.
successCriteria:
- Sheet slides up from bottom and sits above keyboard (not hidden behind it)
- Uses env(keyboard-inset-height) for keyboard-aware positioning on iOS and Android
- Single-line input with placeholder 'What just happened?'
- Character counter visible at 100+ chars, max 120
- Save button calls onSave(text, currentTimestampMs), dismisses sheet
- Cancel button dismisses, discards
- Swipe-down gesture dismisses, discards
- No haptic on save
- Recording (MediaRecorder) continues without interruption during sheet interaction
implementation:
TODO:
1. components/recording/AddNoteSheet/AddNoteSheet.tsx: props { open, onSave: (text: string, timestampMs: number) => void, onClose: () => void, currentTimestampMs: number }. Uses CSS `env(keyboard-inset-height)` via inline style or Tailwind arbitrary value for bottom positioning. Internal state: text (string). Save button disabled if text.trim() === ''.

decisions.decision: env(keyboard-inset-height) not a BottomSheet — custom positioning
decisions.rationale: The shared BottomSheet component positions itself fixed at the bottom of the viewport. With a keyboard open, 'bottom of viewport' is behind the keyboard on many browsers. env(keyboard-inset-height) (part of the CSS Visual Viewport API, supported on iOS 15.4+, Chrome 110+) allows the sheet to sit above the keyboard exactly. This cannot be achieved with the generic BottomSheet component without adding keyboard-awareness to it.
decisions.recommendation: AddNoteSheet is a custom component that implements its own keyboard-aware positioning. Generic BottomSheet not used here.

knownLimitations: env(keyboard-inset-height) not supported on iOS < 15.4 or Chrome < 110 — sheet may appear partially behind keyboard on older browsers
120-char limit is a UX spec requirement — notes longer than 120 chars are not supported
